candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A summary line above finance items. It appears outside the selected COGS bucket ('Materials and services' + 'Changes in Inventory'), but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales-specific, so assigning it to COGS would be speculative and could misallocate a company-wide operating D&A amount.
Component-level D&A row beneath the D&A section. Not clearly COGS-specific, and a broader summary row ('Depreciation & Amortization') exists, so this should not be selected separately.
Balancing row, not a substantive operating D&A line to add separately.

variables.notes: A separate operating D&A total is visible, so D&A does not appear embedded in the selected COGS rows. However, the excerpt provides no COGS-specific D&A label or split. To avoid arbitrary allocation and double counting, no COGS_DA row is selected.

candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A summary line above finance items. It appears outside the selected XSGA bucket ('Personnel expenses' + 'Other operating expenses'), but it is not labelled as SG&A/admin/marketing/R&D-specific, so assigning the whole amount to XSGA would be speculative.
Component-level D&A row within the broader D&A section. Not clearly SG&A-specific, and a summary D&A row exists, so this should not be selected separately.
Balancing row, not a substantive operating D&A line to add separately.

variables.notes: The statement shows only a single operating D&A section, with no SG&A-specific or R&D-specific D&A row. Because XRD is empty and no dedicated R&D line is visible, there is also no basis to extract any R&D amortization separately. Conservative output is no XSGA_DA selection.
